As a developer, I want to validate that a list of people is not empty before processing it, throwing a BadRequestError if it is.

Person represents an individual identified by a unique integer ID; BadRequestError signals invalid input, and requireNotEmpty is a utility that enforces non-empty person lists by raising BadRequestError when empty.

As a developer, I want a TradeService that can retrieve a specific trade using its unique identifier.
TradeService provides access to individual trades via their ID, enabling retrieval of a specific trade record by matching the provided identifier.
Define a TradeService with an operation to fetch a trade by its ID.
As a developer, I want a TradeService that exposes an operation to retrieve a trade using its unique identifier.
TradeService provides a method to look up a specific trade by its ID, returning the corresponding trade details.
Define a TradeService with an operation to fetch a trade by its ID.
As a developer, I want a TradeService that can retrieve a specific trade using its unique identifier.
TradeService provides access to individual trades, allowing retrieval by a unique trade ID.
Define a TradeService with an operation to fetch trades occurring on or after a given start date.
As a developer, I want a TradeService that provides an operation to retrieve all trades with dates greater than or equal to a specified start date.
TradeService exposes an operation that returns a list of trades occurring on or after the specified start date.
Define a TradeService with an operation to fetch trades within a given date range.
As a developer, I want a TradeService that provides a getTradesAfter operation to retrieve all trades occurring between a start and end date.
TradeService exposes an operation to retrieve all trades where the trade date falls within a specified inclusive start and exclusive end time range.
Define a TradeService with an operation to fetch trades occurring after a given transaction event timestamp.
As a developer, I want a TradeService operation that returns all trades where the transaction event time is on or after a specified startDateTime.
TradeService provides access to trades filtered by transaction event time, returning all trades that occurred at or after a given start timestamp.
Define a Film model with an integer ID and title, plus a FilmApi service with a getFilm operation that retrieves a film by its ID.
As a developer, I want a Film model that has an integer ID and a title string, and a FilmApi service with a getFilm endpoint that returns a film when given its ID.
Film represents a movie with a unique numeric identifier and a title, and FilmApi provides a getFilm endpoint to fetch a film by its ID.
Define a Film model with an integer ID, title string, and release year, plus a FilmApi service to fetch films by ID and year.
As a developer, I want a Film model that stores its unique identifier, title, and release year, along with an API endpoint to retrieve a film using both its ID and release year.
Film represents a movie identified by a numeric ID, titled with a string name, and released in a specific year; the FilmApi service enables retrieval of a film by combining its ID and release year.
Define a Film model with an integer ID, title string, and release year, plus a FilmApi service that retrieves films released after a given year.
As a developer, I want a Film model containing an ID, title, and release year, and a FilmApi service that returns films released after the specified year.

Film represents a movie identified by an integer ID, titled with a string title, and released in a given year; FilmApi provides a getFilm operation that returns films released after the specified year.
Define a Foo service with an operation that returns all trades as a list.
As a developer, I want a Foo service that exposes an operation to retrieve all trades.
Foo provides access to a collection of all trades through the listAllTrades operation.
Define a Trade model and a Foo service with an operation that returns all trades as an array.
As a developer, I want a Foo service that provides a listAllTrades operation returning all trades.
Trade represents a financial transaction, and Foo is a service exposing an operation to retrieve all trades.
Define a Money model with a currency field and a convertMoney operation that converts GBP to any target currency.
As a developer, I want a Money model that includes its currency and a convertMoney function to transform GBP amounts into any specified currency.
Money represents an amount of money with a specific currency, and convertMoney is a service operation that converts GBP-denominated Money into another currency.
Define a RewardsAccountBalance with balance and currencyUnit, and create a convert operation that transforms it between currency units.

As a developer, I want a RewardsAccountBalance model and a convert operation that takes a target currency unit and a rewards account balance, returning the converted balance with the new currency unit.

RewardsAccountBalance represents a rewards account’s monetary value and its associated currency, and the convert operation transforms it to a different currency unit while preserving the balance amount.

Define a CreatedAt type based on Date and a RewardsAccountBalance with balance (a decimal) and currency unit (a string), then expose a service operation to fetch rewards balances for records where the creation date falls within a given range.
As a developer, I want to query rewards account balances filtered by a date range on their creation timestamp, using types that represent dates and monetary values with currency context.

CreatedAt represents a timestamp for when an entity was created, RewardsAccountBalance holds a monetary balance and its currency unit, and RewardsBalanceService provides a method to retrieve all balances for records created between two specific timestamps.

Define a Person model and a VyneQL query type, then create a PersonService with a personQuery endpoint that accepts a VyneQL query string and returns an array of Person records, supporting filtering (equality, membership, pattern matching), summing, and counting capabilities.

As a developer, I want to define a Person model and a VyneQL query type, then expose a personQuery endpoint in PersonService that takes a VyneQL query string as input and returns matching Person records, with built-in support for filtering (==, in, like), aggregating sums, and counting results.

Person represents a basic entity with no required fields; VyneQlQuery is a string-based query language for expressing data requests. PersonService provides a personQuery endpoint that executes VyneQL queries against the Person dataset, enabling filtering (exact match, set membership, pattern matching), numeric aggregation via sum, and result counting.
Define a PersonId as an integer and create a PersonService with a getPerson operation that optionally accepts a person ID.
As a developer, I want to define PersonId as an integer type and expose a getPerson endpoint that can optionally take a person ID.
PersonId represents a unique identifier for a person as an integer, and PersonService provides a getPerson method that retrieves a person by their optional ID.
Define a PersonId as an integer and create a PersonService with a getPerson operation that accepts it.
As a developer, I want to define PersonId as an integer and expose a getPerson operation in PersonService that takes a PersonId.
PersonId represents a unique identifier for a person as an integer, and PersonService provides a getPerson operation to retrieve a person by their ID.
Define a PersonId as an integer and create a PersonService with a getPerson operation that accepts a person’s ID.
As a developer, I want to define PersonId as an integer type and expose a getPerson endpoint in PersonService that takes a person’s ID as input.
PersonId represents a unique identifier for a person encoded as an integer, and PersonService provides a getPerson operation to retrieve a person by their ID.
Define a Person model and a PersonService with two operations that both return lists of Person objects.
As a developer, I want a Person model and a PersonService that can retrieve all persons via two equivalent operations named findAll and find.
Person represents an individual entity, and PersonService provides methods to fetch collections of persons using either findAll or find operations.
Define a Person model and a PersonService with an operation to find people, optionally filtered by whether they are alive.
As a developer, I want a Person model and a PersonService that can retrieve a list of people, defaulting to only living individuals.
Person represents an individual entity, and PersonService provides a method to query people—by default returning only living persons.
Define a Person model and a PersonService with an operation to find people whose names start with "JIM" by default.
As a developer, I want a Person model and a PersonService operation that returns people whose names start with "JIM" unless another starting string is provided.
Person represents an individual, and PersonService provides a findPeople operation that returns a list of people whose names begin with "JIM" by default, or a specified prefix when provided.

description: Define a Person model with a required name and optional spouse name, where both names are strings, and create a PartialPerson variant that inherits from Person.
As a developer, I want to model a person with their name and an optional spouse’s name (both as strings), plus a partial version of the model for flexible usage.
Person represents an individual with a full name and an optional spouse’s name, both stored as strings; PartialPerson is a flexible variant of Person that can omit fields.
Define a Pet model with name and parent fields, a Person model with name, pet, and deadPets fields, and a PartialPerson partial model derived from Person.

As a developer, I want to define models for pets and people where each pet has a name and an optional parent (also a pet), each person has a name, one current pet, and a list of deceased pets, plus a partial version of Person for flexible updates.

Pet represents an animal with a name and an optional parent (another pet), Person represents a human with a name, one current pet, and a list of past pets, while PartialPerson enables partial updates to Person records.
Define a Person model with name (a string), spouse (another person), and friends (a list of people), plus a partial version for optional fields, and a SpouseName type that’s just a string alias.
taxi:
type SpouseName inherits String
            model Person {
               name : Name inherits String
               spouse : Person
               friends : Person[]
            }
            partial model PartialPerson from Person

taxi:

            model Person {
               name : Name inherits String
               spouse : Person
               friends : Person[]
            }
            partial model PartialPerson from Person

description: Person represents an individual with their full name, a reference to their spouse (who is also a person), and a list of friends; SpouseName is a specialized string type for spouse names, and PartialPerson allows partial updates to a person’s data.
taxi:
type SpouseName inherits String
            model Person {
               name : Name inherits String
               spouse : Person
               friends : Person[]
            }
            partial model PartialPerson from Person

description: Define a Person model with a name field derived from Name (which inherits String), and a PartialPerson variant that omits null fields.
As a developer, I want a Person model with a name field based on Name (a String subclass), plus a PartialPerson version that excludes null values.
Person represents an individual with a name, and PartialPerson is a variant that omits null fields for more compact representations.
Define a Person model with a name field that inherits from String, and allow partial instances of it.
As a developer, I want a Person model with a name field derived from String, and the ability to create partial Person records.
Person represents a person with a name, supporting both full and partial representations for flexible data handling.
Define a Contacts model with an email field and a Person model that contains contacts, plus a PartialPerson variant allowing partial data.
As a developer, I want to model a person with an email address and support partial updates for that person.
Contacts holds a single email address, Person encapsulates contact information for an individual, and PartialPerson enables representing incomplete person records.
Define a Film model and grant external read access to all films.
As a developer, I want a Film model with public read access so external systems can retrieve film data.
Film represents a cinematic work with publicly accessible read permissions for external consumers.
Define a Film model with no fields and grant external read access to all films.
As a developer, I want a Film model that anyone can read externally.
Film represents a cinematic work accessible for external reading without restrictions.
Define an Account model linked to a user ID, and restrict read access so users can only view their own accounts.
As a developer, I want an Account model tied to a user ID and a policy that ensures users can only read accounts they own.
Account represents a user-specific resource tied to a unique identifier, with access control ensuring users can only retrieve their own account data.
Define a Film model with title and yearReleased fields, where yearReleased can be null when reading.
As a developer, I want to define a Film entity with a title and an optional release year, allowing queries to omit the yearReleased field.
Film represents a movie with its title and optional release year, supporting read operations where the release year may be unspecified.
Define an AccountsPolicy that restricts read access to accounts only when the user’s security groups include ADMIN or COMPLIANCE.
As a developer, I want to enforce that users can only read accounts if they belong to at least one of the ADMIN or COMPLIANCE security groups.
AccountsPolicy ensures that account data is only returned to users whose access tokens include ADMIN or COMPLIANCE in their assigned security groups.
Define a Film model with a title field derived from String and a policy that blocks all read access to Title.
As a developer, I want a Film model with a title field that inherits from String and a policy that prevents any read access to Title.
Film represents a movie with a title, and the TestPolicy ensures no read access is ever granted to Title values.

Define a Film model with a title field that inherits from String, and add an uppercase extension function for strings along with a policy that attempts to read uppercase titles (though the condition always fails).

As a developer, I want to define a Film model with a title field derived from String, provide an uppercase function for strings, and create a policy that tries to return "foo" when reading titles (but never actually does).

Film represents a movie with a title, which is a string-like value; the uppercase function converts strings to their all-caps form, and TestPolicy is a read-only policy for titles that currently blocks access by always evaluating to null.
Define a HitFilm model that extends Film and includes a title field derived from the film's title converted to a HitFilm type.
As a developer, I want a HitFilm model that inherits from Film and has a title field created by converting the film's title into a HitFilm value.
HitFilm represents a specialized type of film derived from the base Film model, where its title is constructed by transforming the original film's title into a HitFilm-specific format.
Define a Film model with a title field and a derived hit field that converts the title to a HitFilm using a cast function.
As a developer, I want a Film model that includes a title and automatically computes its hit version by casting the title to HitFilm.
Film represents a movie with a title and an associated hit version, where the hit is derived by casting the film's title to HitFilm.
Define a generic `nowLike()` function and a `PeopleService` with a `findPeople` operation that accepts an optional HTTP header for filtering people modified in the last day.
As a developer, I want a generic function to get the current time and a PeopleService endpoint that uses an If-Modified-Since header (defaulting to yesterday’s date) to return a list of people.

The `nowLike()` function returns the current time in a generic type, and `PeopleService.findPeople` retrieves people whose records were modified since a given timestamp—defaulting to 24 hours ago—if provided via the HTTP header.
Define an Actor model with a table of actors and annotate the service method with documentation and a custom annotation.
As a developer, I want an Actor model backed by a table of actors and a service method annotated with typedoc and a custom annotation.
Actor represents a performer or participant in a system, stored as a table of records and managed by a service method annotated with documentation and a custom marker.
Define an Actor model and a service to manage a table of actors.
As a developer, I want an Actor model with a service that provides access to a table of actors.
Actor represents an individual who can perform actions, and ActorService provides access to a collection of actors stored in a table.
Define an Actor model and a service that exposes a stream of actors.
As a developer, I want an Actor model with a service that provides a stream of actors annotated with custom metadata.
Actor represents an entity in the system, and ActorService exposes a real-time stream of all actors for consumption by clients.

An empty data structure that can be extended with fields in the future.

Person represents an individual with a verified email address, where the email is stored as a distinct Email type derived from String.

Person represents an individual with a contact email address; Animal represents a creature categorized by its biological species.
Define a Person model with an email field and an Author model with a name field.
As a developer, I want to define Person and Author types, each with a single string-based field (email for Person, name for Author).
Person represents an individual identified by their email address; Author represents a writer identified by their name.
Define a Person model with an email field that is a specialized string type.
As a developer, I want a Person model that includes an email field represented as a distinct Email type derived from String.
Person represents an individual with a contact email address, where the email is modeled as a dedicated Email type based on String.

As a developer, I want a Film model that includes a unique film identifier represented as a string.
Film represents a movie entry identified by a unique film ID derived from a string value.
Define a Film model with filmId and title fields, both derived from String.
As a developer, I want a Film model that has a filmId and a title, each represented as a string.
Film represents a movie entry with a unique identifier (filmId) and its title, both stored as strings.
Define a Film model with a filmId field that serves as its unique identifier and is based on the String type.
As a developer, I want a Film model that has a filmId field serving as its unique identifier and derived from String.
Film represents a movie with a unique identifier (filmId) that is fundamentally a string value.
Define a Person model with a personId field derived from String, and a VipPerson subtype; provide a service to fetch all people and update a person.
As a developer, I want a Person model with an identifier field and a VipPerson extension, plus a service to list all people and update individual records.
Person represents an entity with a unique identifier, extended by VipPerson for privileged users; PersonService enables retrieval of all people and modification of existing records.
Define an UpdatePeople query that retrieves all Person records and invokes the updatePerson function on the PersonService.
As a developer, I want an UpdatePeople query that fetches all people and calls PersonService::updatePerson to apply changes.
UpdatePeople is a query that fetches all person records and triggers the updatePerson method on PersonService to persist modifications.
Define a query named UpdatePeople that invokes the updatePerson method on PersonService without any input parameters.
As a developer, I want a query called UpdatePeople that calls PersonService::updatePerson with no arguments.
UpdatePeople is a query that triggers the updatePerson operation on PersonService, though it currently lacks any input data to perform the update.
Define an UpdatePeople query that accepts a person ID and invokes the PersonService updatePerson method.
As a developer, I want an UpdatePeople query that takes a person ID and calls the PersonService updatePerson endpoint.
UpdatePeople is a query that accepts a specific person identifier and triggers the updatePerson operation on the PersonService.

As a developer, I want to define a MovieId type derived from String and a query operation that retrieves movies using the movie ID passed as a path variable in a GET request to /reviews/{movieId}.
MovieId represents a unique identifier for a movie, implemented as a string. The MoviesAndReviews query fetches movies by matching the provided movie ID from the URL path against stored records.

As a developer, I want to look up a movie and its associated reviews using a string-based movie identifier.
MovieId is a unique identifier for a film represented as a string; the MoviesAndReviews query fetches a specific movie along with all its reviews based on that identifier.

Define a MovieId as an integer and create a query to find a movie with ID 123.
As a developer, I want to define a MovieId type based on integers and write a query that retrieves the movie with ID 123.
MovieId represents a unique identifier for a movie as an integer, and FindMyMovie retrieves the specific movie with ID 123.
Define a MovieId as an integer and fetch the movie with ID 123.
As a developer, I want to define MovieId as an integer type and retrieve the movie whose ID is 123.
MovieId represents a unique identifier for a movie as an integer, and the query retrieves the specific movie with ID 123.

Define a Film model with filmId and title fields, and a Musical model with musicalId, plus a query that converts an array of Film objects into an array of Musical objects by mapping each film’s fields to corresponding musical fields.
As a developer, I want to define Film and Musical data structures and a conversion function that transforms a list of films into musicals by extracting their IDs and titles.

Film represents a movie with a unique identifier and title; Musical represents a musical production identified by its own ID. The convertFilm query transforms a collection of films into musicals, preserving the original film’s ID and title while assigning them to the musical structure.
Define a Person model with a name field that inherits from String, and create aliases for HumanName as Name and Human as Person.
As a developer, I want a Person model with a name field derived from String, plus HumanName and Human as convenient aliases for Name and Person respectively.
Person represents an individual with a name, where HumanName is a reusable type alias for Name and Human is an alternative name for Person.
Define a Person model with first and last name fields derived from strings, and a greeting function that takes both names to produce a string.
As a developer, I want a Person model that includes first and last name fields (as strings) and a greeting computed from them using the hello function.
Person represents an individual with a first name and last name, along with a greeting message generated by passing both names to the hello function.
Define a Person model with first and last name fields derived from strings, plus a greeting field initialized by calling the generic hello function.
As a developer, I want a Person model that includes first and last name fields (as strings) and an automatically computed greeting field using the hello function.
Person represents a person with a first name, last name, and a greeting message generated by the hello function.

description: Define a Puid type and a PUID enum with one variant, FxSpot, representing a numeric identifier in the 100–2000 range.
As a developer, I want to define a Puid type and a PUID enum containing FxSpot(919) to represent a numeric product identifier within the 100–2000 range.

Puid is a numeric identifier, typically between 100 and 2000, used to uniquely identify financial products; PUID is an enumeration with a single value, FxSpot(919), representing the specific identifier for foreign exchange spot transactions.

Define a PagingObject with a limit, extend it to PagingTrackObject that includes an array of Track items, define SpecialIdList as a specialized list of public track IDs, and link Tracks to their external IDs via SpecialIdList within a SearchResult that holds paginated tracks.

As a developer, I want to model search results that include paginated track lists, where each track has external IDs stored in a specialized list type, and the pagination includes a limit and an array of track items.

SearchResult represents a paginated collection of tracks, where each page includes a limit and an array of Track objects; each Track has external IDs stored in a SpecialIdList, which is a specialized list of PublicTrackId values derived from strings.
Define a Name model with firstName and lastName fields, each inheriting from NamePart (a string), and a Person model with name and friends (an array of Person).
As a developer, I want to model people with their full name (split into first and last parts) and a list of friends, where each friend is also a person.

Name represents a person's full name composed of first and last parts, each derived from a common string-based NamePart; Person represents an individual with their full name and a list of other people they are friends with.
Define a Track model with an artist object containing id, genres, and latest release album ID, plus its own album ID.
As a developer, I want a Track model that includes artist details (id, genres, and latest release album) and its associated album ID.
Track represents a musical recording linked to an artist (with their identifier, genre list, and latest album) and the specific album it belongs to.
Define a Film model with an IMDb score (as a decimal) and a Catalog model containing a list of films.
As a developer, I want to represent films with their IMDb scores and group them into catalogs.
Film represents a movie with its IMDb rating, and Catalog is a collection of films.
Define a Person model with a name field, a FilmStudio model with films and videos arrays of films, and a Film model with a team containing actors and crew arrays of people.
As a developer, I want to model Person (with a name), FilmStudio (with lists of films and videos), and Film (with a team of actors and crew members) to represent a film production ecosystem.
Person represents an individual with a name; FilmStudio represents a company that produces or distributes films and videos; Film represents a movie with an associated team of actors and crew members.
Define two stream types—tweets and analytics—and two queries that each consume a union of Tweet or TweetAnalytics events.
As a developer, I want to define stream services for tweets and analytics, plus two queries that each subscribe to a combined stream of either tweet or analytics events.
JoinedStreamsA and JoinedStreamsB are queries that subscribe to a unified stream of mixed tweet and analytics events, enabling real-time processing of both data types together.
Define a Tweet model and a TweetAnalytics model, then define ThingA and ThingB each with a field that holds either a Tweet or a TweetAnalytics.
As a developer, I want ThingA and ThingB to each have a field that can store either a Tweet or its associated analytics data.
ThingA and ThingB each represent entities capable of associating a tweet with its analytics, allowing flexible storage of either the tweet itself or its performance metrics.
Define a Tweets service with two streams—tweets and analytics—and a query that merges them into a single stream of mixed Tweet or TweetAnalytics events.
As a developer, I want to subscribe to a unified stream that emits both tweets and their analytics so I can process them together in real time.
JoinedStreamsA represents a real-time stream that interleaves tweet posts and their corresponding analytics data, enabling unified handling of both event types.
Define a Tweet model and an Analytics model, then create a union type that can hold either one.
As a developer, I want to define separate models for tweets and analytics data, and combine them into a single type that accepts either.
TweetAndAnalytics represents a value that may be either a tweet or analytics data, allowing flexible handling of mixed content.
Define a TweetAndAnalytics union type that can hold either a list of tweets or a list of analytics records.
As a developer, I want to represent data that is exclusively either a collection of tweets or a collection of analytics entries.
TweetAndAnalytics represents a choice between a sequence of tweets and a sequence of analytics records, ensuring type-safe handling of mutually exclusive data structures.
Define a Tweet model with an integer ID and an Analytics model with its own distinct integer ID, then create a query that returns either a list of tweets or a list of analytics records.
As a developer, I want to define separate models for tweets and analytics—each with their own integer-based ID—and a query that fetches either tweets or analytics in a single call.

Tweet represents a social media post identified by an integer ID, Analytics represents engagement metrics also keyed by an integer ID, and MyTestQuery retrieves a mixed list of either tweets or analytics records.
Define a Tweet model and an Analytics model, then combine them into a new type that includes both.
As a developer, I want to define separate Tweet and Analytics models and then create a combined type that represents data from both.
TweetAndAnalytics represents the union of tweet content and its associated analytics metrics, combining data from both sources into a single cohesive structure.
Define a TweetAndAnalytics query that streams both Tweet and Analytics data together.
As a developer, I want to stream tweets alongside analytics data in real time using a single query.
TweetAndAnalytics represents a combined stream of tweets and their associated analytics events for real-time processing.
Define a Tweet model with an integer ID and user reference, a User model with a userId field, and a query that streams both Tweet and User records.

taxi:
namespace acme.fx

[[
ISO currency codes are the three-letter alphabetic codes that represent the various [currencies](https://www.investopedia.com/terms/i/isocurrencycode.asp) used
throughout the world. When combined in pairs, they make up the symbols and [cross rates](https://www.investopedia.com/terms/c/crossrate.asp) used in currency
trading.

Each of the country-specific three-letter alphabetic codes also have a
corresponding three-digit numeric code. These codes are identified by the
[International Organization for Standardization (ISO)](https://www.investopedia.com/terms/i/international-organization-for-standardization-iso.asp) a nongovernmental organization
which provides standards for manufacturing, commerce, technology and communication.

For currencies, the governing document is called ISO [4217:2015](https://www.iso.org/standard/64758.html).
]]
type alias CurrencySymbol as String


[[
In the forex market, currency unit prices are quoted as currency pairs. The base currency – also
called the transaction currency - is the first currency appearing in a currency pair quotation,
followed by the second part of the quotation, called the quote currency or the counter currency.
For accounting purposes, a firm may use the base currency as the domestic currency or accounting currency
to represent all profits and losses.

## Breaking down Base Currency
In forex, the base currency represents how much of the quote currency is needed for you to get one unit of the base currency. For example, if you were looking at the CAD/USD currency pair, the Canadian dollar would be the base currency and the U.S. dollar would be the quote currency.
]]
type BaseCurrency inherits CurrencySymbol

[[
The quote currency, commonly known as "counter currency,"

including non-financial institutions. They enable a legal party to a financial transaction to be identified precisely.
It links back to a data set of critical information about the transacting entity, which can also include information on the ultimate ownership of the entity.
Source: [Swift](https://www.swift.com/standards/data-standards/lei)

]]
type  LegalEntityIdentifier inherits String

[[
What a party is known as.     
]]
type PartyName inherits String

As a developer, I want to define a Country enum for common nations and two models—Person (with first name, last name, and country of residence) and CsvPerson (with first and last names mapped from CSV columns)—to support structured data and CSV import.

Country represents a set of supported nations (New Zealand, Australia, United Kingdom); Person holds a person’s first name, last name, and country of residence; CsvPerson mirrors Person’s structure but explicitly maps its fields to named columns in a CSV file.

description: Define PersonId as an integer identifier for a person, PersonName and EmailAddress as strings representing a person’s name and contact email; define PetId, Name, and Tag for pets; define ReleaseYear, FilmTitle, Genre, and Rating for films; define AlbumTitle, ArtistName, ReleaseYear, and Genre for music albums.

As a developer, I want distinct typed identifiers and fields—like PersonId (int), PersonName (string), EmailAddress (string) for people; PetId, Name, Tag for pets; ReleaseYear, FilmTitle, Genre, Rating for movies; and AlbumTitle, ArtistName, Genre for music—so I can avoid ambiguous string/int usage across domains.

PersonId uniquely identifies a person using an integer; PersonName holds their name as text; EmailAddress stores their email address. PetId uniquely identifies a pet, Name holds its name, and Tag categorizes it. ReleaseYear records the year a film was released; FilmTitle holds its title; Genre classifies it; Rating assigns a numeric score. AlbumTitle names the album; ArtistName identifies its creator; ReleaseYear marks its release year; Genre classifies its musical style.
Define a PersonId as an integer and a PersonName as a string, omitting EmailAddress to cause Avro compilation failure.
As a developer, I want PersonId and PersonName types based on integer and string respectively, intentionally excluding EmailAddress to trigger Avro schema validation errors.
PersonId represents a person’s unique numeric identifier, and PersonName holds their full name as text; EmailAddress is intentionally omitted to ensure Avro schema generation fails.
Define PersonId as an integer identifier, and PersonName and EmailAddress as string fields for a person's name and email.

type PersonName inherits String
type EmailAddress inherits String

description: Define a Kafka service named KafkaService connected to "my-kafka-connection".
As a developer, I want a KafkaService that connects to the Kafka cluster configured under "my-kafka-connection".
KafkaService represents a service endpoint integrated with Apache Kafka using the named connection "my-kafka-connection".
Define a Person model with an ID field derived from String and a query that casts it to Long
As a developer, I want a Person model with an ID field that inherits from String and a find query that converts it to Long
Person represents an individual identified by a unique string-based identifier, with a query that retrieves the ID as a Long value
Define a FilmListWrapper containing an array of FilmBundles, each wrapping a single Film with a title field.
As a developer, I want to query for a list of films wrapped in bundles so I can retrieve an array of film titles.
FilmListWrapper holds a collection of FilmBundle objects, each containing one Film with a title represented as a Title (a specialized string).
Define a Person model with a name field and a Movie model with a cast array, plus a query that extracts the first person’s name from the cast.
As a developer, I want to model people and movies, and query movies to retrieve the name of the first person in their cast.
Person represents an individual with a name; Movie represents a film with a list of cast members. The query retrieves the names of the first cast member for each movie.

Define a Deal with an ID and borrower reference, group deals into ExistingDeals, and expose APIs to fetch a single deal or all deals for a borrower; also support querying existing deals associated with a given deal’s borrower.

As a developer, I want to model deals with IDs and borrowers, bundle multiple deals into an ExistingDeals container, provide endpoints to retrieve a specific deal or all deals for a borrower, and query existing deals tied to a deal’s borrower.

Deal represents a financial agreement identified by an integer ID and linked to a borrower via their ID. ExistingDeals groups multiple deals together, and DealApi offers operations to fetch a single deal by ID or all deals for a borrower. A query can retrieve existing deals associated with the same borrower as a given deal.
Define a Film model with an integer ID and a query to fetch films along with their associated revenue records.
As a developer, I want to retrieve a list of films, each enriched with its corresponding revenue data linked by film ID.
Film represents a movie identified by a unique integer ID, and the find query retrieves films alongside their revenue information by matching film IDs to revenue records.
Define a Deal with an ID and borrower ID, and an ExistingDeals container for deals grouped by borrower; then find a specific deal and retrieve all other deals for the same borrower.
As a developer, I want to look up a deal by ID and fetch all other deals associated with that borrower.

Deal represents a financial agreement identified by a unique ID and linked to a borrower; ExistingDeals groups all deals for a given borrower, enabling queries that retrieve related deals when examining a specific deal.

Define Film and FilmReview models with integer and string IDs respectively, and a ReviewWrapper that links them; then define a query to fetch films along with their associated reviews by matching film IDs.

As a developer, I want to model films and reviews with distinct ID types, wrap reviews with their IDs, and query films enriched with their corresponding reviews by joining on matching film identifiers.

Film represents a movie identified by an integer ID, while Review represents a user-submitted review identified by a string ID; ReviewWrapper associates a review ID with its full review data, and the query retrieves all films along with their matching reviews by linking film IDs to review IDs through the wrapper.

Define Film and FilmReview models with integer and string IDs respectively, and a ReviewWrapper that holds a FilmReview; then define a query to fetch films along with their associated reviews by matching film IDs.
As a developer, I want to model films and reviews with distinct ID types, wrap reviews for lookup, and query films enriched with their matching reviews using film ID as the join key.

Film represents a movie identified by an integer ID; Review represents a user review identified by a string ID and is wrapped for lookup; the query retrieves all films with their corresponding reviews by linking film IDs to review IDs through the wrapper.
Define a Customer model with id and name fields, and a query to find specific customers by their IDs.
As a developer, I want a Customer model that has an id and name, plus a query to fetch specific customers when given a list of IDs like ["c1", "c2"].
Customer represents a person with a unique identifier and full name, and the find query retrieves a list of customers matching provided IDs.
Define a Customer model with an ID and name, and a query to fetch customers excluding IDs "c1" and "c2".
As a developer, I want to define a Customer entity with an ID and name, and retrieve all customers except those with IDs "c1" or "c2".
Customer represents a person with a unique identifier and full name, and the find query retrieves all customers except those whose IDs are explicitly listed as "c1" or "c2".
Define a list of allowed countries (NZ and AU) with their currency codes, then query for all countries matching a given list of currency codes.
As a developer, I want to define countries with their currency codes and query for countries whose currency codes match a provided list.
Countries represent supported regions (New Zealand and Australia) each associated with a currency code, and the query retrieves all countries whose currency codes match those in an input list.
Define a list of allowed countries (Australia and New Zealand) and query for all valid country entries from that list.
As a developer, I want to specify a predefined set of countries (Australia and New Zealand) and retrieve all valid country values from that set.
A list of supported countries includes Australia (AUD) and New Zealand (NZD); the query returns all entries in this list.
Define a list of supported countries (New Zealand and Australia) with their currency codes, and provide a query to retrieve them.
As a developer, I want to define supported countries with their currency codes and query them as a list.
Supported Countries represents the two active markets—New Zealand and Australia—with their respective currency codes, and a query allows fetching this list for use in pricing or localization logic.

Define an ErrorDetails record with numeric code and string message, then define BadRequest and Unauthorized as specific error instances, and query to extract the numeric code from a given BadRequest instance.

As a developer, I want to define structured error details with code and message fields, create BadRequest and Unauthorized error variants, and retrieve the numeric HTTP status code from a given error instance.

ErrorDetails represents an HTTP error with a numeric status code and human-readable message; BadRequest (400) and Unauthorized (401) are predefined error instances, and the query extracts the code from a given BadRequest error.
Define a Person model with a nested names object containing a firstName field of type FirstName, and create a query to extract the person's first name.
As a developer, I want a Person model that includes a firstName field nested under names, and a query that returns the person's first name as a separate field called name.
Person represents an individual with at least a first name, stored under a names object; the find query retrieves and exposes the person's first name as a top-level field named name.
Define a Film query that returns each film’s title and the name of its headliner actor, looked up from the cast list.
As a developer, I want to fetch films along with their headliner’s name by matching the film’s headliner ID against its cast list.
A Film query that retrieves each film’s title and the name of its headliner actor, resolved by finding the cast member whose ID matches the film’s headliner field.
Define Film and Actor models with title, headliner, and cast fields, then query films to return each film’s title and the name of its headliner by matching cast members’ IDs to the headliner ID.
As a developer, I want to fetch films along with the name of their headliner actor by looking up the headliner’s ID in each film’s cast list.

Film represents a movie with a title, a headliner actor ID, and a list of cast actors; the query retrieves each film’s title and the name of its headliner by resolving the headliner ID against the cast list.

Define a Film model with title, headliner actor ID, and cast list, and a query that finds films and retrieves the headliner’s name and film title by matching cast members to the headliner using a custom actor ID mapping.
As a developer, I want to query films and fetch the headliner’s name by matching their ID against the cast list using a transformed actor ID structure that includes an IMDb ID field.

Film represents a movie with a title, a lead actor (headliner), and a list of cast members; the query retrieves each film along with its headliner’s name by resolving the headliner ID to a cast member via an extended actor identifier that incorporates IMDb IDs.

Define a Movie model with a cast of actors, an Actor model with a name field derived from string, and a Movies service with a findOne operation that returns a single movie; also define a query to find movies starring an actor named 'Mark'.

As a developer, I want to model movies and actors with a cast relationship, provide a way to fetch one movie at a time, and query movies by actor name to get only those where the specified actor appears in the cast.

Movie represents a film with an associated list of actors; Actor represents a performer identified by their name, which is a string value; the Movies service exposes a method to retrieve a single movie; and a query allows filtering movies by a specific actor’s name to return only those where that actor is in the cast.

Define a Movie model with a cast of actors, an Actor model that extends string for names, and a Movies service with a findOne operation; also define a query to find movies starring actors matching a given name using a custom filter function.

As a developer, I want to model movies and actors (where an actor’s name is just a string), provide a way to fetch one movie, and write a query that filters a movie’s cast by a specific actor name using a generic filter function.

Movie represents a film with an array of actors in its cast; Actor represents a performer whose identity is captured by a name (inheriting from string); the Movies service exposes a findOne operation to retrieve a single movie; additionally, a query is defined that accepts an actor name and returns movies where the cast includes that actor, using a generic filtering mechanism on the cast array.

Define a toUpper function that converts a string to uppercase and use it in a query to transform "hello" TASK: As a developer, I want a toUpper function that transforms any string to uppercase so I can use it in queries like transforming "hello" DOC: The toUpper function takes a string and returns its uppercase equivalent, demonstrated here by converting the literal "hello" to "HELLO"
Define a function that converts a string to uppercase and use it in a query to transform the input message.
As a developer, I want a function that uppercases a given string and a query that applies it to a default "hello" message.
A utility function converts text to uppercase, and a query demonstrates its use by transforming the default message "hello" into uppercase.
Define string extension functions toUpper and toLower, then use them in a query to convert "hello" to uppercase and back to lowercase.
As a developer, I want to add case-conversion utility functions to strings and apply them in sequence to transform "hello" into uppercase then lowercase.
String case conversion utilities that allow transforming text to all caps or all lowercase, demonstrated by converting the string "hello" through uppercase then back to lowercase.
Define a Movie model with a title field derived from String, and create a service operation to retrieve all movies, plus a query that filters for the movie titled "Jaws".
As a developer, I want to model movies with a title, fetch all movies via a service method, and query specifically for the movie titled "Jaws".
Movie represents a film with a title, and the Movies service exposes an operation to list all movies; additionally, there's a query that returns only the movie titled "Jaws".
Define a Movie model with a title field derived from String, and a Movies service that streams all movies; also define a query to stream only movies titled "Jaws".
As a developer, I want a Movie model with a title field based on String, a Movies service to stream all movies, and a query that filters and streams only movies titled "Jaws".
Movie represents a film with a title, the Movies service provides a stream of all movies, and a query filters that stream to include only entries where the title equals "Jaws".
Define a lookup function for enum values by name and declare BadRequest/Unauthorized error details with numeric codes and messages.
As a developer, I want to look up predefined error details (like BadRequest or Unauthorized) by their string name and retrieve structured code/message pairs.
ErrorDetails represents an error with a numeric status code and human-readable message; Errors is an enum of standard HTTP errors, each associated with a specific code and message.
Define a filter function for arrays and use it to find movies where any cast member’s name is Mark.
As a developer, I want to filter an array of actors using a name-based condition so that I can extract movies starring 'Mark'.
The filter function takes a collection and a predicate to return matching elements; the query finds movies whose cast includes an actor named Mark.
Define a User model with username and roles, then query to determine if the user is an admin or guest based on their assigned roles.
As a developer, I want to define a User with a username and list of roles, and query whether that user has ADMIN or GUEST privileges.
User represents a person with a unique username and a list of assigned roles; the query checks if the user holds ADMIN or GUEST permissions to determine access level.
Define a StockReport model with quantity (integer) and lastUpdated (date formatted as dd/MM/yyyy), and a query that returns an array of StockReport objects with lastUpdated formatted as MM-yy.
As a developer, I want a StockReport model containing quantity and lastUpdated fields, plus a query that fetches stock reports with the lastUpdated field formatted as MM-yy.

StockReport represents inventory data with a count of items and the date it was last updated, formatted as dd/MM/yyyy; the find query retrieves a list of such reports with the update date displayed in MM-yy format.
Define a concat function that joins multiple strings and use it to compute 'abc' in a query.
As a developer, I want a concat function that takes any number of strings and returns their concatenation, so I can build dynamic string values like 'abc' in queries.
concat combines a variable number of strings into a single string; the query uses it to produce the value 'abc' by joining 'a', 'b', and 'c'.
Define a function that accepts an optional integer count (defaulting to 1) and a variable number of strings, returning a concatenated string.
As a developer, I want to call `mixedDefaultWithVarargs` with one required integer and multiple string arguments to produce a combined string result.
mixedDefaultWithVarargs constructs a string from an optional count parameter (defaulting to 1) and any number of additional string arguments.

Define a generic joinUp extension function for string arrays with optional separator, prefix, and postfix parameters, and demonstrate its use to join an array of strings into a single comma-separated string.
As a developer, I want to join an array of strings into one string using a customizable separator and optional prefix/postfix, so I can easily format lists for display or export.

joinUp concatenates an array of strings with a configurable separator (defaulting to a comma) and optional prefix and postfix, returning the combined string; for example, joining ['a', 'b', 'c'] produces "a,b,c".

Define a Person model with first and last names and an address, where the address includes a house number and street name, and add a query that returns people with their names and addresses annotated with whether the house number is odd.
As a developer, I want to model people with their names and addresses, and query them to include whether each person’s house number is odd.

Person represents an individual with a first name, last name, and full address; Address comprises a house number and street name. The find query retrieves people alongside their names and addresses, including a computed field indicating if the house number is odd.
Define an Entity model with a type field and a query that finds all entities of type "Person".
As a developer, I want an Entity model that includes a type field and a query to retrieve all entities where the type is "Person".
Entity represents any kind of entity with a specified type, and the query retrieves all entities explicitly typed as "Person".

Define a Person model with first and last names and an address, where the address includes a house number and street name, and add a query that returns people with their names, addresses, and an indicator of whether the house number is odd.
As a developer, I want to model people with their names and addresses, and query them to include whether the house number is odd.

Person represents an individual with a first name, last name, and full address; Address comprises a house number and street name. The find query retrieves people along with their names, addresses (including whether the house number is odd), and all other address fields.

Define a Person model with first and last names, an address (including house number and street name), and a secret age; expose a query that returns people with their names, addresses (excluding the secret code), and a computed field indicating whether the house number is odd.

As a developer, I want to model people and their addresses—including house numbers, street names, and secret codes—while also tracking a person’s age in secret—and query people with their full names, address details (but not the secret code), and a boolean flag for odd-numbered houses.

Person represents an individual with a first name, last name, full address (including house number and street name), and a confidential age. The find query retrieves people with their names, address details (excluding the secret code), and a computed field that indicates whether the house number is odd.
Define a Person with name and address details, excluding streetName and secretCode, and include valuation and last purchase price for the address.
As a developer, I want to query Person records with their name and address (excluding streetName and secretCode), along with the property’s valuation and last purchase price.

Person represents an individual with a full name and residential address; the associated address includes valuation and last purchase price, omitting sensitive or redundant fields like street name and secret code.

Define a Person model with an integer ID and name, a Movie model with a cast of People, and a query that returns movies whose cast members’ names contain the letter 'a', excluding IDs from the filtered results.

As a developer, I want to model People and Movies where each Movie has a cast of People, and I need a query that finds all movies whose cast includes at least one person whose name contains the letter 'a', returning only their names (without IDs).

Person represents an individual with a unique numeric identifier and a name; Movie groups People who appear in it. The query filters movies to include only those with cast members whose names contain the letter 'a', returning a list of such people (excluding their IDs).

Define a Person model with an ID and name, a Movie model with a cast of People, and a query that returns movies whose cast members contain the letter 'a' in their name, enriched with blood type and credit score while excluding the person's ID.

As a developer, I want to retrieve movies and their cast members who have the letter 'a' in their name, including each qualifying person’s blood type and credit score—but not their ID—so I can analyze demographic or risk-profile data.

Person represents an individual with a unique identifier and full name; Movie groups people who appear in it as cast members. The query filters cast members by whether their name contains the letter 'a', then augments each match with blood type and credit score while omitting the person’s ID.

Define a Person model with an integer ID and name, a TwitterHandle string type, and a Movie model with a Person star; then define a query that fetches movies and their cast members’ Twitter handles nested under socials.

As a developer, I want to model Person and Movie entities where each movie has a starring person, and I need a query that retrieves movies along with their cast’s Twitter handles nested in a socials object.

Person represents an individual with a unique numeric ID and full name; TwitterHandle is a string type for social identifiers; Movie represents a film starring one or more people, and the find query retrieves movies with their cast’s Twitter handles grouped under a socials object.
Define a Message type based on String, a StockQuote model with ticker, price, and quantity fields, and a stream that emits StockQuote records with a fixed message.

As a developer, I want to define a Message as a string-like type, a StockQuote model with ticker symbol, price, and quantity fields, and a stream that emits StockQuote instances alongside a static message.

Message is a string-based type for conveying textual information; StockQuote represents market data with a ticker symbol, current price, and trade quantity; the stream emits StockQuote records augmented by a default message.
Define a Message as a string-like type, a StockQuote model with ticker, price, and quantity fields, and a query that returns stock quotes alongside a fixed message.

As a developer, I want to define a Message type based on strings, a StockQuote model with ticker symbol, price, and quantity fields, and a query that fetches stock quotes while including a default message.

Message represents a string-based communication payload; StockQuote captures market data for a security, including its ticker symbol, current price, and available quantity; the find query retrieves stock quotes and pairs each with a static greeting message.
Define a Message as a string and a StockQuote with ticker, price, and quantity fields; then define a query that returns a StockQuote excluding the ticker field.
As a developer, I want to represent messages as strings and stock quotes with ticker symbol, price, and quantity; and retrieve a stock quote without exposing its ticker in the result.

Message is a string-based representation of communication content. StockQuote holds financial instrument data including its ticker symbol, current price, and available quantity. The find query retrieves a stock quote while omitting the ticker field from the output.
Define a Message as a string, a StockQuote with ticker, price, and quantity fields, extend it to BuySideQuote, and define a query that returns a BuySideQuote excluding the ticker field.
As a developer, I want to model stock quotes with ticker, price, and quantity, allow buy-side quotes to inherit those fields, and query for a quote without exposing the ticker.

Message represents a generic string-based communication; StockQuote captures market data including the security identifier, current price, and trade size; BuySideQuote extends StockQuote for buy-side-specific quoting; the query retrieves a buy-side quote while omitting the ticker field from the response.
Define a Message type as a string, a StockQuote model with ticker, price, and quantity fields, extend it with BuySideQuote, and define a query that returns a quote along with its message.

As a developer, I want to model stock quotes with ticker symbol, price, and quantity, allow buy-side quotes to inherit those fields, and support querying a quote alongside an associated message string.

Message represents a textual communication encoded as a string; StockQuote captures market data including the ticker symbol, price, and quantity for a trade; BuySideQuote extends StockQuote to represent buy-side market interest; the find query retrieves a quote and its associated message.
Define a HumanId as a string-based identifier and a Person model with a personId field, then create a query that accepts a humanId, casts it to PersonId, and retrieves the matching Person.
As a developer, I want to define human-readable IDs as strings and look up a Person by casting a given humanId into a personId to fetch the corresponding record.

HumanId represents a human-readable identifier implemented as a string, and Person is a model with a unique personId field; the MyQuery query takes a humanId, converts it to a PersonId, and retrieves the associated Person record.
